evidence: count_domains() is defined independently in analysis/tmd_count.py, analysis/non_srp_filter.py, analysis/srp_filter.py, and analysis/Uniprot_TMD_search.py — four copies of identical logic, indicating a missing shared boundary

calculate_cterm_distance() in analysis/Uniprot_TMD_search.py and calc_min_cterm_distance() in protein_analysis_utils.py are semantically equivalent functions that duplicate C-terminal distance logic across modules
analysis/analysis_test.py redefines filter_and_compare() inline — a local copy of the function that already exists in protein_analysis_utils.py

IDR/idr_analysis.py imports requests and uses it for UniProt API calls; analysis/Uniprot_TMD_search.py does not use requests but performs similar domain extraction — no shared boundary for data-fetch vs. analysis concerns
app/app.py and STANDALONE_APP.py both independently load the dataset at module level from the same GitHub URL — duplicated data-access boundary with no shared loader module
No __init__.py files in analysis/ or IDR/ — directories act as namespaces but lack formal package declarations, causing test files to rely on sys.path manipulation to import them

strengths: protein_analysis_utils.py is a clear central utility module that analysis scripts import from, establishing a reasonable hub boundary
IDR/ is well-separated as a specialist subsystem with its own requirements.txt and does not import from analysis/
app/ is cleanly separated from analysis logic — it imports only from the GitHub data URL, not from analysis scripts
cross_module_architecture.issue_character: The dominant issue is cross-package function duplication: count_domains and cterm_distance logic are copied verbatim across multiple analysis scripts instead of being consolidated in protein_analysis_utils.py, creating a codebase-wide blast radius for any logic change.
cross_module_architecture.score_rationale: The codebase has a sensible three-layer shape (app / analysis / IDR) but violates its own implied boundary by scattering identical utility functions across scripts. The count_domains duplication alone spans four files. The missing __init__.py files and sys.path hacks in tests signal that the package structure is informal. These are not severe cycles or hub explosions, but the duplicated boundary logic meaningfully degrades modularity — placing this in the lower-middle range.

issues.summary: count_domains() defined identically in four separate modules
evidence: analysis/tmd_count.py lines 12-15: def count_domains(domain_str: str | float) -> int: ... return len(re.findall(r'\d+\.\.\.\d+', str(domain_str)))
Identical function body in non_srp_filter.py, srp_filter.py, and Uniprot_TMD_search.py
issues.suggestion: Move count_domains() to protein_analysis_utils.py (or a new analysis/domain_utils.py), then import it in all four files

issues.summary: C-terminal distance calculation duplicated as calculate_cterm_distance and calc_min_cterm_distance
evidence: Uniprot_TMD_search.py defines calculate_cterm_distance(row) which iterates over Transmembrane and Intramembrane columns
protein_analysis_utils.py defines calc_min_cterm_distance(row) which iterates only over Transmembrane — semantically overlapping but different signatures and scope
issues.suggestion: Unify into a single function in protein_analysis_utils.py that accepts an optional list of domain columns; import from there in Uniprot_TMD_search.py

issues.summary: analysis/analysis_test.py redefines filter_and_compare() already present in protein_analysis_utils.py
evidence: analysis_test.py defines a local filter_and_compare() with partial operator support inside main()
protein_analysis_utils.py exports the canonical version with full operator set
issues.suggestion: Remove the local definition in analysis_test.py and import from protein_analysis_utils instead; this file appears to be a superseded exploratory script

root_causes: Analysis scripts were written independently before protein_analysis_utils.py existed, and utility functions were never backported/consolidated
Absence of formal package __init__.py files makes cross-directory imports awkward, encouraging copy-paste over import
